ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'stuffed pepper food product' class; no specific jalapeño relleno entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated jalapeño relleno code. Closest defensible match is stuffed fried chilli pepper composite. Code A0CKM used as closest parent anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for jalapeño rellenos as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 6 pieces (approximately 250 g), as listed on the GrabFood menu, representing a shared appetiser or individual snack portion.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Jalapeno peppers, stuffed with cheese, breaded, fried (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite estimate)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry exactly matches 'Chile Jalapeño Rellenos (6 pcs)' as a composite dish. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for jalapeño peppers (FDC ID 170930), cream cheese (FDC ID 172179), cheddar cheese (FDC ID 173414), breadcrumbs (FDC ID 172822), and frying oil absorption factors. The input record's per_100g values (energy 300 kcal, protein 10 g, fat 22 g, carbohydrate 16 g) are consistent with this composite and are retained with medium confidence. Secondary validation against USDA Branded Food entries for commercial jalapeño poppers confirms plausibility.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283. — analogous estimate based on breaded fried vegetable snack / chilli pepper composite
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for jalapeño rellenos. GI of 45 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries: breaded fried vegetable snacks and stuffed pepper dishes typically fall in the low-to-medium GI range (GI 40–55) due to the protein and fat matrix from cheese slowing glucose absorption. The breadcrumb coating contributes refined carbohydrate but the overall fat and protein load suppresses the glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as: GI (45) × carbohydrate per serving (38.4 g from 16 g/100 g × 240 g edible portion) / 100 = 17.3. Carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 40 g; edible portion estimated at 240 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 480 mg/100 g; a 250 g serving delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium, representing 52–60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit intake to 1–2 pieces per occasion and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods; baked preparation with reduced-sodium cheese would meaningfully lower the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate content (16 g/100 g, ~40 g per serving) with low GI (~45) partially offset by 2 g/100 g dietary fibre and 10 g/100 g protein from cheese, but high saturated fat (10 g/100 g) may impair insulin sensitivity with regular consumption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes may consume 2–3 pieces occasionally as part of a balanced meal; the low GI and protein content moderate the glycaemic response, but the high saturated fat content warrants limiting frequency to avoid long-term insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 10 g/100 g (50 g per 250 g serving exceeds the daily limit of 20 g), trans fat at 0.2 g/100 g, cholesterol at 48 mg/100 g, and sodium at 480 mg/100 g collectively represent a significant cardiovascular risk burden.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence only, limiting to 1–2 pieces, and preferring baked or air-fried preparation to reduce saturated fat, trans fat, and AGE formation associated with deep-frying.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); saturated fat at 10 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g threshold; jalapeños are nightshade vegetables that may trigger flares in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried jalapeño rellenos; if consumed, baked preparation with reduced-fat cheese is preferred, and those sensitive to nightshade vegetables should avoid jalapeños entirely.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chile Jalapeño Rellenos (6 Pieces) is a Mexican-origin appetiser or snack consisting of six whole jalapeño chilli peppers that are halved or left whole, stuffed generously with a mixture of cream cheese and cheddar cheese, then coated in a seasoned breadcrumb batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy. The dish delivers a characteristic contrast of textures — a crunchy, golden exterior giving way to a molten, creamy cheese filling with the fiery heat of the jalapeño. The jalapeño provides moderate capsaicin-driven heat, vitamin C (12 mg/100 g), and dietary fibre (2 g/100 g), while the cheese filling contributes significant protein (10 g/100 g), calcium (120 mg/100 g), and saturated fat (10 g/100 g). The deep-frying process raises the total fat content to 22 g/100 g and energy to 300 kcal/100 g, and generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs). A standard serving of 6 pieces (approximately 250 g) delivers around 750 kcal, 1200 mg sodium, and 25 g protein. Clinically, this dish is high concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium and saturated fat load, moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to its moderate carbohydrate content and low GI (~45), and high concern for rheumatoid arthritis due to deep-frying and nightshade content. It is suitable for vegetarians but not vegans, and contains gluten, dairy, and egg allergens. This dish is commonly served as a party appetiser, bar snack, or side dish in Mexican and Tex-Mex cuisine contexts, and appears on food delivery platforms in Singapore as a fusion snack item.

confidence_reason: No exact USDA FoodData Central entry exists for jalapeño rellenos as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy component entries for jalapeño peppers, cream cheese, cheddar, breadcrumbs, and frying oil absorption, and are consistent with the input record's per_100g values. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite estimation basis.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries (jalapeño pepper FDC 170930, cream cheese FDC 172179, cheddar FDC 173414, breadcrumbs FDC 172822) as no single approved-source entry for jalapeño rellenos exists. Input per_100g values retained as consistent with composite estimate. 2. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient breakdown for this composite deep-fried stuffed jalapeño dish is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-16; component-level data exists but composite totals are not published. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (7.5) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (2.5) are estimated from the fatty acid profile of cream cheese, cheddar, and frying oil (canola/vegetable oil blend) per USDA SR Legacy; these are approximate. 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for jalapeño rellenos is published in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16. 5. Input ethnic_tags included 'Naan' and 'Burrito' which are inconsistent with this dish; these have been removed. Country of origin corrected from 'Singapore' to 'Mexico' as jalapeño rellenos are a Mexican-origin dish served in Singapore. 6. Halal status is not confirmed as the cheese sourcing and frying oil are not certified halal in the GrabFood listing context. 7. All aliases_by_language string values are present verbatim in the flat aliases array (q8 self-check passed).
